name: Don Elliott
abstract: Don Elliott Helfman (October 21, 1926 – June 5, 1984), known as Don Elliott, was an American jazz trumpeter, vibraphonist, vocalist, and mellophone player. Elliott recorded over 60 albums and 5,000 advertising jingles throughout his career.

concerts.label: Connie Francis, Singer, and Special Guests
New York Jazz Repertory Company: The Music of Quincy Jones

about_quotes.speaker: Rusty Dedrick
about_quotes.evidence: he picked me and Don Elliott to be the players. And the rhythm section was known at that time as the NBC rhythm section... And we just went in and played.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rusty Dedrick interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Orrin Keepnews
about_quotes.evidence: I went to a couple of these funky clubs in Greenwich Village where Bill was playing in quartets with somebody like Tony Scott or Don Elliot and got to know Bill
about_quotes.doc_title: Orrin Keepnews interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Bertoncini
about_quotes.evidence: And all the dates with Don Elliott in recording dates on Madison Avenue, it was always Mel Lewis and Richard Davis and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gene Bertoncini interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Danko
about_quotes.evidence: Don Elliot started playing there... And so I started playing there
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Danko interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 28, 2021
